methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $15M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Housing & Construction (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 1 same-sector peers exist in Housing & Construction, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Inversion Space
private.rationale: Same sector (Housing & Construction), same stage (Series A)

private.name: Cobod
private.rationale: Same sector (Housing & Construction), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Material Evolution is a Series A–stage housing and construction company founded by Liz Gilligan and Sam Clark that has raised $15M to date. The relevant peer universe in Housing & Construction comprises three private companies (ICON, Inversion Space, Cobod); no public comparables exist in the immediate sector.

The stage-progression method is the applicable triangulation approach here. Using total capital raised ($15M) as a valuation proxy for a Series A company and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier—consistent with typical Series A to subsequent-stage progression—yields an implied valuation of $30M. A peer-median approach could not be applied due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comps in the narrow Housing & Construction peer set; a broader cross-sector median would introduce material bias and is therefore excluded.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $26M (low) to $34M (high), with a median of $30M. The narrow methodological foundation—relying on a single applicable method—reflects genuine data constraints rather than analytical choice. Key uncertainties include the absence of public market benchmarks, limited visibility into private peer valuations, and the early-stage revenue profile typical of frontier-construction companies. Any valuation within the stated range should be treated as preliminary pending deeper diligence on unit economics, technology defensibility, and management track record.
